bug_report.summary: Invalid Source-tab parsing dialog is now overly generic (loses explicit “Source tab/Bib(La)TeX source” context and guidance)
steps_to_reproduce: Create an Article entry and open its entry editor
Go to the “BibTeX source” tab and make the entry syntactically invalid (e.g., delete the final closing brace '}')
Switch to another entry to trigger parsing/apply on focus loss
expected_behaviors: Error dialog should clearly indicate the problem is caused by invalid Bib(La)TeX in the Source tab (context-specific title/message).
Dialog should provide user-oriented guidance similar to the previous wording (e.g., pointing to wrong syntax in the source).
actual_behaviors: Dialog title changed from context-specific (“SourceTab error”) to generic (“Error Occurred”).
Dialog text no longer explicitly references the Source/BibTeX source tab and drops the prior user-oriented explanation/guidance, showing mainly a low-level parse error message.

bug_report.summary: “SourceTab error” dialog does not dismiss (or immediately reappears) after clicking OK, blocking interaction
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new library and add an entry; open the entry editor.
Open the “Source” tab and make the Bib(La)TeX invalid (e.g., delete the final closing brace ‘}’).
Switch to another tab to trigger the error handling dialog.
Click the dialog’s “OK” button (to close it) and attempt to continue (e.g., return to the Source tab).
expected_behaviors: Clicking “OK” should close the modal dialog and return control to the entry editor so the user can continue editing/fixing the source.
actual_behaviors: After the code change, the “SourceTab error” dialog remains visible after clicking “OK” (or is immediately shown again), continuing to block the entry editor view.

bug_report.summary: No user-facing notification/dialog shown when leaving Source tab with invalid Bib(La)TeX content
steps_to_reproduce: Add a new entry (e.g., PhdThesis) and open the entry editor.
Replace the entire source with plain text that is not a Bib(La)TeX entry (no @type{...} block).
Click another tab (e.g., “Required fields”) to move focus away from the source editor.
expected_behaviors: A user-facing notification/dialog should appear indicating the Bib(La)TeX source is invalid (instead of silently accepting it or surfacing an exception).
Invalid source changes should not be applied to the entry.
actual_behaviors: The UI switches to the other tab, but no invalid-Bib(La)TeX notification/dialog is shown in the after-change UI state.
